This is about understanding model internals for performance improvement, not about AI safety or alignment. It is not related to frontier models or general reasoning, so it falls under Class 2 (Truthfulness, Reliability & XAI) as it improves the trustworthiness of metrics for a specific artifact.

title: Do Not Train It: A Linear Neural Architecture Search of Graph Neural Networks
reasoning: The paper focuses on improving the efficiency and performance of neural architecture search for Graph Neural Networks through a no-update scheme and sparse coding, with no mention of safety, ethics, truthfulness, or alignment. Its primary goal is advancing general capabilities (speed and accuracy) rather than addressing any safety concerns, placing it firmly in Class 3.

title: Mechanistic Mode Connectivity
reasoning: The paper studies neural network loss landscapes through mode connectivity, providing a definition of mechanistic similarity and demonstrating that linear connectivity indicates shared mechanisms. It proposes connectivity-based fine-tuning (CBFT) to alter mechanisms, advancing general understanding of model internals and steering. This fits Class 4 under the Interpretability subdomain, as mechanistic interpretability of general neural networks is inherently safety-relevant, and it is not a narrow domain-specific application.
